metric_basis.true_positive: positive gold tests with a complete case-specific semantic match
metric_basis.false_positive: negative-control violations plus reportable findings not semantically matched to any positive gold test
metric_basis.false_negative: positive gold tests without a complete semantic match

checks.name: fact_anchor
checks.detail: finding references water-restriction fact F018

checks.name: both_instructions
checks.detail: finding states both 'no water' and 'only water/water allowed' instructions

checks.name: logical_relation
checks.detail: finding identifies the two instructions as contradictory

checks.name: human_gate
checks.detail: finding requires human review

evidence.message: Water restriction is described inconsistently: Protocol Synopsis and SAP state 'no water is allowed one hour before and one hour after dosing' (F018), while Protocol Body states 'only water allowed one hour before and one hour after dosing' (F018). These are logically contradictory — one prohibits water, the other mandates it. The conflict affects participant burden description (ICF-PARTICIPATION) and safety interpretation.

checks.name: fact_anchor
checks.detail: finding references design F005 and dose-day F007

checks.name: boundary_values
checks.detail: finding states both the >3-day claim and exact three-day boundary

checks.name: numeric_relation
checks.detail: finding explains why the numeric claims disagree

checks.name: human_gate
checks.detail: finding requires human review

evidence.message: The protocol claims a washout interval greater than 3 days (>3 days), but dose Days 1, 4, 7, and 10 provide exactly 3 days between doses. The exact boundary does not satisfy the strict greater-than claim.

checks.name: fact_anchor
checks.detail: finding references duration fact F009

checks.name: duration_values
checks.detail: finding states the 11-day and about-12-day descriptions

checks.name: definition_relation
checks.detail: finding treats this as a definition/counting distinction

checks.name: not_hard_conflict
checks.detail: finding does not overstate an unambiguous hard conflict

evidence.message: Protocol/SAP describe an 11 day study or confinement duration, while participant-facing ICF language says about 12 days. Qualified review must reconcile the counting definition and confinement language.

checks.name: fact_anchor
checks.detail: finding references screening-window fact F008

checks.name: window_values
checks.detail: finding states the protocol 30-day and ICF 31-day boundaries

checks.name: boundary_relation
checks.detail: finding explains the inclusive-boundary or participant-language distinction

evidence.message: Protocol screening is no more than 30 days (<=30), while the ICF says up to 31 days. Qualified review must reconcile the inclusive boundary and participant-facing wording difference.

evidence.reason: no finding semantically reports this valid distinction as a conflict

checks.name: population_anchor
checks.detail: finding references the PP/evaluable population facts

checks.name: population_concepts
checks.detail: finding relates population mapping to the SAP safety-only scope

checks.name: expert_review
checks.detail: finding defers mapping/merge to qualified review

evidence.message: SAP-DESIGN states 'The SAP is limited to safety analysis' (F024), yet SAP-PK-SPEC and PROT-POPULATIONS define and rely on Evaluable Population (F021) for dose proportionality and food-effect analyses — primary objectives (F013). No section reconciles how PK analyses (core to study objectives) are accommodated within a safety-limited SAP, nor clarifies whether PK analysis rules originate from SAP or Protocol.

evidence.reason: no finding semantically reports this valid distinction as a conflict

checks.name: all_facts
checks.detail: trace finding covers F001 through F027

checks.name: trace_fields
checks.detail: trace includes source_ids, source_locator, and status

checks.name: complete_trace
checks.detail: finding explicitly asserts complete source traceability

evidence.message: Complete source traceability is present for the fact register: each fact has source_ids, each source has a source_locator, and each fact has status.

checks.name: old_new_relation
checks.detail: one or more F015 findings explicitly relate 32 to 36 hours

checks.name: affected_classes
checks.detail: covered=['crf', 'icf', 'protocol', 'registry_results', 'sap', 'schedule_of_activities']; required=['crf', 'icf', 'protocol', 'registry_results', 'sap', 'schedule_of_activities']

checks.name: original_preserved
checks.detail: F015 keeps 32-hour previous_value and proposes 36 hours in version 2+

checks.name: human_approval
checks.detail: F015 proposals remain gated for qualified human approval

checks.name: formal_document_gate
checks.detail: run requires qualified decisions/review before formal release

checks.name: proposal_provenance
checks.detail: every candidate proposal carries fact_ids and evidence_source_ids
